§6Heavy Oil§f

§aThe most abundant fraction from crude oil — your gateway to lubricants, bitumen, and industrial oil!§f

Heavy Oil is the most common product from the §bOil Refinery§f. Every §e1,000mB§f of hot crude oil yields §e500mB§f of Heavy Oil, making up half of all refinery output. It is a dark, viscous liquid with low fuel value (§e55 kTU§f / §e68.7 kHE§f per bucket) and is best processed further rather than burned directly.

§6Production§f:
§b• Oil Refinery§f (primary): §e500mB§f per §e1,000mB§f hot crude oil.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: Process §bLiquid Hydrogen§f + Coal or Coal Powder → §e1,000mB§f Heavy Oil.
§b• Compressor§f: Compress §bBlood§f → §e250mB§f Heavy Oil (at §e3 PU§f).

§6Uses§f:
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: §e500mB§f Heavy Oil → §e150mB§f Bitumen + §e350mB§f Industrial Oil (the primary processing path).
§b• Chemical Plant§f: §e2,000mB§f Heavy Oil → §e1,000mB§f Engine Lubricant.
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e11,000mB§f Heavy Oil → §e1×§f Petroleum Coke + §e1,100mB§f Coker Oil.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e500mB§f Heavy Oil + Coal/Cool Powder/Petroleum Coke → §e1,000mB§f Coal Gasoline.
§b• Industrial Solidification Machine§f: §e150mB§f → §e1×§f Oil Tar.

§eTip§f: Heavy Oil is renewable! §bDesulfurized Crude Oil§f from the refinery outputs a different ratio: §e300mB§f Heavy Oil per bucket. The §bFractioning Tower§f is your main tool for upgrading Heavy Oil into more valuable products.
